options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
The win condition for this game.

- labyrinth_open: Open both Grey Labyrinth entrances using Radiant Projectors.
- newbucks: Accumulate a lifetime Newbucks threshold (set by Newbucks Goal Amount).
- prismacore_enter: Enter the Prismacore area for the first time.
- prismacore_stabilize: Complete the main story — stabilize the Prismacore.
- slimepedia: Unlock all 29 Slimes entries AND all 54 Resources entries in the Slimepedia.

options.name: newbucks_goal_amount
options.description:
Lifetime Newbucks required to complete the Newbucks goal.
Uses lifetime total (unaffected by spending), so farming is always progress.
Only relevant when Goal is set to newbucks.

options.name: randomize_pods
options.description: Include the 140 Treasure Pods as location checks.

options.name: randomize_gordos
options.description:
Include the 17 static Gordo Slimes as location checks.
Grey Labyrinth Gordos are only included for the prismacore_enter, prismacore_stabilize, and slimepedia goals.

options.name: randomize_map_nodes
options.description:
Include the 15 Map Data Nodes as location checks.
Grey Labyrinth nodes are only included for the prismacore_enter, prismacore_stabilize, and slimepedia goals.

options.name: randomize_slimepedia
options.description:
Include the 29 Slimepedia Slimes category entries as location checks.
Forced on when Goal is slimepedia.

options.name: randomize_slimepedia_resources
options.description:
Include the 54 Slimepedia Resources category entries as location checks.
Covers all food types (veggies, fruits, hens/chicks), craft materials,
and special resources (Moondew Nectar, Snowball, Prisma materials).
Forced on when Goal is slimepedia.

options.name: randomize_slimepedia_radiant
options.description:
Include the 22 Radiant Slimepedia entries as location checks.
A Radiant Slime entry is unlocked the first time the player catches a radiant
variant of that slime type. Radiant slimes are rare random world spawns;
use Radiant Spawn Rate Multiplier to increase how often they appear.

options.name: randomize_research_drones
options.description:
Include the 23 Research Drones (Gigi's journal drones) as location checks.
Each drone has a main entry (approaching the drone) and an archive page (reading its second page).
The Drone Archive Key becomes a required key item to access the archive page checks.

options.name: randomize_ghostly_drones
options.description:
Include the 10 Ghostly Drones (collectible mechanical drones) as location checks.
Grey Labyrinth drones are only included for the prismacore_enter, prismacore_stabilize, and slimepedia goals.

options.name: randomize_puzzle_doors
options.description:
Include the 28 plort doors (PuzzleSlotLockable, lockTag='plort_door') as location checks.
These are the coloured plort-locked doors found throughout Ember Valley (4), Rainbow Fields (1),
Starlight Strand (12), and the Grey Labyrinth (11). The Powderfall Bluffs region gate door
is NOT included here — it is handled separately by the Region Access Mode setting.
Grey Labyrinth shadow plort doors (depositor doors) are also NOT included — those are
always in the pool when the Grey Labyrinth is in scope (goal: prismacore/slimepedia).

WARNING: This feature is not yet implemented in the mod. Enabling it will add locations to
the pool that can never be checked, which will cause the game to be uncompletable.
Leave this off until a future update.

options.name: conversation_checks
options.description:
Controls which CommStation conversations count as location checks.

- none: No conversations are location checks. All ranchers give vanilla gifts directly.
- conditional: The 10 conversations with confirmed zone or chain access conditions.
  Includes the full rancher intro-call chain (Viktor/Mochi/Ogden/Thora), Viktor's Radiant
  Projector Blueprint, Thora's Gordo Snare Advanced, Mochi's Archive Key, and BOb's intro
  call, first gift, and story call (BOb requires visiting Ember Valley and Starlight Strand).
- all: Every conversation — decorative gifts, story/lore, and deflect dialogue (~120 total).
  WARNING: Only recommended for async (asynchronous) multiworld games. Many conversations
  are gated behind real-world time delays of 1–3 in-game days each, and the full chain
  of all conversations can take many play sessions to complete. In a synchronous game
  where other players are waiting on your checks, this mode is likely to become a
  bottleneck. Use `conditional` for synchronous play.

options.name: region_access_mode
options.description:
Controls how region gate switches are handled.

- vanilla: Region gates work as in the base game — activating a switch opens the zone
  immediately. No region access items are added to the pool.
- locations: Gate switches become blocked location checks. The corresponding region gate
  will not open until the matching Region Access item is received from the item pool.
- bundled: Same as locations, but also grants the corresponding zone teleporter blueprint
  automatically when the Region Access item is received.

options.name: disable_tarr
options.description:
When enabled, Tarr slimes (traps and checks) are removed from the randomizer entirely:
- The Slimepedia Tarr entry is excluded from the location pool (even when
  Randomize Slimepedia is on).
- Tarr Spawn Trap and Tarr Rain Trap are removed from the trap pool regardless
  of their individual weight settings.

Use this if you prefer to play without Tarr.

options.name: tarr_instakill
options.description:
When enabled, Tarr bites always kill the player instantly regardless of current health
or the Incoming Damage Multiplier setting.
Off by default — Tarr deal their normal 20 base damage (scaled by the multiplier).

options.name: incoming_damage_multiplier
options.description:
Multiplier applied to all incoming damage the player receives.
1 = normal damage (default).
2 = double damage.
5 = five times normal damage.
Note: a Tarr bite deals 20 base damage. At 5x this equals 100, which kills the player
at default health — but is survivable with Health Tank upgrades.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: force_heavy_weather
options.description:
When enabled, weather events always spawn at their maximum severity (Heavy tier).
Light and Medium weather phases are skipped — patterns jump directly to Heavy as
soon as they start.
Flat single-state patterns (Slime Rain, Snow) are unaffected.
On by default — ensures players can reliably farm the zone-specific resources
that only appear during severe weather (Slime Rain spawns, snow-exclusive items, etc.).

options.name: weather_frequency_multiplier
options.description:
Multiplier applied to weather event frequency.
SR2 schedules weather events every 0.25–1.0 in-game hours by default.
This option divides that interval by the multiplier, making weather changes start more often but last for less time

1 = vanilla interval (default).
2 = weather starts twice as frequently.
4 = weather starts four times as frequently.

options.name: all_radiant_slimes
options.description:
When enabled, every slime encounter spawns as a radiant variant — the shuffle-bag
algorithm is bypassed entirely and DrawFromRadiantShuffleBag always returns true.

If this is on, Radiant Spawn Rate Multiplier has no additional effect and can be
left at its default value of 1.

Only relevant when Randomize Slimepedia (Radiant Slimes) is enabled; harmless otherwise.

options.name: radiant_spawn_rate_multiplier
options.description:
Multiplier applied to radiant slime spawn frequency.
SR2 uses a shuffle-bag algorithm: each slime type has a bag of N encounters;
when the bag empties the next encounter with that type is guaranteed radiant.
This option divides all bag sizes by the multiplier, making radiant slimes
appear proportionally more often.

1 = vanilla rates (default). Typical bag sizes range from 25 (Yolky) to 2000 (Pink).
2 = radiant slimes appear ~2× as often.
5 = radiant slimes appear ~5× as often.
10 = radiant slimes appear ~10× as often.
50 = radiant slimes appear ~50× as often (near-guaranteed on every other encounter;
     bag sizes are floored at 2 so a radiant is never certain on every spawn).

Has no effect when All Radiant Slimes is enabled.
Only relevant when Randomize Slimepedia (Radiant Slimes) is enabled; harmless otherwise.

options.name: gold_lucky_spawn_rate_multiplier
options.description:
Multiplier applied to Gold and Lucky slime spawn weight.
SR2 uses a weight-based system: each DirectedActorSpawner has a SlimeSet where each
member has a Weight value. Gold and Lucky slimes have very small weights (typically 1–5)
compared to common slimes (50–200+), making them rare.
This option multiplies all Gold and Lucky member weights by the given value.

1 = vanilla weights (default).
2 = Gold/Lucky weights doubled — roughly 2× more frequent.
5 = Gold/Lucky weights ×5 — noticeably more common.
10 = Gold/Lucky weights ×10 — frequent encounters in most zones.
35 = Gold/Lucky weights ×35 — very common; note that Gold slimes drop large amounts
     of Newbucks, so high values will significantly accelerate the economy and may
     trivialize the Newbucks goal.

options.name: start_with_resource_harvester
options.description:
When enabled, the Resource Harvester upgrade is granted at the start of the game
instead of being shuffled into the item pool.
The Resource Harvester allows the player to gather craft materials from resource nodes.
Useful for players who find early crafting progression gating frustrating.

options.name: trap_percentage
options.description:
Percentage of filler item slots that are replaced with trap items.
Set to 0 to disable traps entirely.
Which trap types appear and how often is controlled by the individual Trap Weight options.

options.name: tarr_spawn_trap_weight
options.description:
Relative weight of the Tarr Spawn Trap in the trap pool.
Tarr Spawn places two Tarr directly next to the player.
Set to 0 to exclude this trap type entirely.

options.name: teleport_trap_weight
options.description:
Relative weight of the Teleport Trap in the trap pool.
Teleport sends the player to a random accessible zone entrance.
Set to 0 to exclude this trap type entirely.

options.name: tarr_rain_trap_weight
options.description:
Relative weight of the Tarr Rain Trap in the trap pool.
Tarr Rain triggers the Slime Rain weather event, but Tarr fall from the sky instead of slimes.
Set to 0 to exclude this trap type entirely.

options.name: vacpack_spew_trap_weight
options.description:
Relative weight of the Vacpack Spew Trap in the trap pool.
Vacpack Spew launches all items currently in the player's vacpack as physical world objects
and clears every slot — everything you were carrying flies out at once.
Set to 0 to exclude this trap type entirely.

options.name: vacpack_fill_trap_weight
options.description:
Relative weight of the Vacpack Fill Trap in the trap pool.
Vacpack Fill fills every empty slot in the player's vacpack with Pink slimes to capacity.
Set to 0 to exclude this trap type entirely.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
